composition.Programmer 1:
Implement the backend code in app.py
- Develop the main backend Python application using Flask.
- The website should be accessible via local port 5000, with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard page).
- Implement routing for all nine frontend pages, ensuring each route corresponds to the frontend templates.
- Implement file read/write operations for all data files in the 'data' directory, starting from the first line, using plain text without encryption.
- Manage all business logic including browsing restaurants, viewing menus, managing cart, placing orders, tracking deliveries, and handling reviews.
- Frontend templates to be supported  dashboard.html, restaurants.html, menu.html, item_details.html, cart.html, checkout.html, active_orders.html, track_order.html, reviews.html.
- Incorporate the original task’s supplementary condition  no authentication required; all features directly accessible.
composition.Programmer 2:
Develop all frontend HTML templates in the 'templates/' directory
- Complete the frontend code for all nine pages as per the detailed page designs.
- Ensure all URLs in the frontend code are Flask routes, not direct HTML file names.
- Implement the page elements with specified IDs and types for each page 
  - dashboard.html (Dashboard Page)
  - restaurants.html (Restaurant Listing Page)
  - menu.html (Restaurant Menu Page)
  - item_details.html (Item Details Page)
  - cart.html (Shopping Cart Page)
  - checkout.html (Checkout Page)
  - active_orders.html (Active Orders Page)
  - track_order.html (Order Tracking Page)
  - reviews.html (Reviews Page)
- Follow the page titles, overviews, and element specifications exactly as defined in the requirements document.
composition.Programmer 3:
Develop static assets in the 'static/' directory
- Create CSS styles in css/styles.css for consistent styling across all pages.
- Develop JavaScript in js/scripts.js to handle client-side interactivity such as button actions and dynamic filtering.
- Organize and include images (restaurant logos, menu item photos, etc.) in images/ folder.
- Ensure static assets support and enhance the user experience of all frontend pages.
